input: Before we add support for exporting a workspace's full discussion history as a shareable link, can you run a security review?
expected_behavior: Skill activates. Reads spec/features/security.feature and spec/contracts/agent-contract.yaml, considers the new shareable-link feature against each STRIDE category (e.g. Spoofing/Information Disclosure risk from a link that bypasses the workspace-URL-as-secret model), and proposes an update to spec/threat_model.md rather than only giving a prose opinion.
rubric: Explicitly walks through STRIDE categories relevant to the new feature
References spec/features/security.feature and/or agent-contract.yaml as the existing inventory rather than starting from a generic STRIDE checklist
Proposes a spec/threat_model.md update (new or revised subsection) rather than only a prose answer
Does not propose fixing anything in test or implementation files in the same step

input: I'm adding a new agent tool that writes structured notes to Firestore on the user's behalf.
expected_behavior: Skill activates even though the word 'security' never appears — a write-capable tool is a new trust boundary (this project's tools are currently read_only/no_write_tools per agent-contract.yaml) and an Elevation of Privilege concern. The skill should flag this as spec-relevant and walk through the STRIDE implications before any implementation.
rubric: Triggers without the word 'security' appearing in the input
Identifies the write capability as breaking the existing no_write_tools/read_only constraint in agent-contract.yaml
Frames the concern using Elevation of Privilege (or another correct STRIDE category), not just generic caution
Does not proceed to implement the tool without first surfacing the threat-model implications

input: I extracted the block-lookup logic in discussion-agent/app/document_search.py into a small private helper function for readability. No behavior change.
expected_behavior: This skill does NOT activate — a pure refactor introduces no new trust boundary, tool, or untrusted-input source. This is spec-sync's territory (checking whether any spec update is needed at all, which in this case it correctly is not), not a STRIDE review.
rubric: No STRIDE category walkthrough or spec/threat_model.md update is proposed
A pure refactor with no new boundary does not trigger this skill
Response addresses the refactor directly, deferring any spec-sync judgment to that skill instead
